NALCO & NLC INDIA SIGN JV FOR 1,080 MW CAPTIVE POWER PLANT ⚡🏭

• 🤝 NALCO and NLC India have signed a 50:50 Joint Venture Agreement to develop a 1,080 MW (4×270 MW) captive thermal power plant at Angul, Odisha

• ⚙️ The project will supply 100% captive power for NALCO's 0.5 MTPA aluminium smelter expansion

• 📜 The JV will sign a 25-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with NALCO, while NLC India will provide coal through a long-term Fuel Supply Agreement

• 🌱 The partners will also explore 200–250 MW of firm renewable energy to meet Renewable Consumption Obligation (RCO) requirements

• 🏢 The JV will be incorporated under the Companies Act, with NALCO and NLC India each holding a 50% stake

🟢 Impact: Positive (The JV secures long-term captive power for NALCO's expansion while strengthening NLC India's power development and fuel supply business.)

DECCAN CEMENTS: CRISIL DOWNGRADES RATINGS AFTER LOAN CLOSURE MISCLASSIFICATION ⚠️🏦

• 📉 CRISIL downgraded the company's long-term rating to CRISIL BB (Issuer Not Cooperating) from CRISIL BBB/Stable

• ✅ Company clarified that it has fully repaid all term loans and informed CRISIL on 6 July 2026

🔴 Impact: Negative (Despite the company's clarification, the downgrade to "Issuer Not Cooperating" may weigh on investor sentiment.)

SOUTH KOREAN STOCKS SEE HEAVY FOREIGN SELLING 🇰🇷📉

• 💸 Global equity funds sold ~$8 billion of South Korean stocks so far in July (~$1.6 billion per trading day)

• 📊 Follows ~$30 billion of foreign outflows in June, the largest monthly sell-off on record

• 📉 Year-to-date, foreign investors have sold over $100 billion worth of Korean equities

• 🛒 More than half of the selling has been absorbed by domestic retail investors

• 📉 The KOSPI has entered a bear market, falling 23% from its June 19 intraday peak, marking its biggest decline since 2022

🟡 Market View: Persistent foreign outflows reflect concerns over valuations, semiconductor earnings, and global risk sentiment. A sustained recovery will likely depend on improved chip demand and a return of foreign institutional buying.

Adani Enterprises’ Kutch Copper Limited (KCL) Achieves London Metal Exchange (LME) Brand Registration for ‘Adani Copper’ Grade-A Cathodes.

This enables Adani Copper cathodes to be delivered with warrants eligible for issuance against LME Copper futures contracts from July 10, 2026.

The certification by the world centre for the trading of industrial metals validates KCL’s manufacturing excellence and responsible sourcing practices against strict global benchmarks.

The recognition places Adani Copper alongside the world’s leading copper brands.

Backed by USD 1.2 billion, KCL’s Mundra facility (0.5 million tonnes per annum capacity) is one of the world’s largest single-location custom copper smelting complexes. Equipped with advanced technology and sustainable design principles, it strengthens domestic supply of the metal, reduces India’s reliance on imports and advances the ‘Aatmanirbhar Bharat’ vision.

Kusumgar Limited – Our Take on Post-Listing Prospects

Not a GMP take. A "Will it Hold Up Post-Listing?" take

📌 The Setup

- IPO Size: ₹650 crore
- Price Band: ₹398–419
- Opens: 8 July
- Closes: 10 July
- Listing: 15 July
- Anchor Book: ₹193.95 crore from 23 institutions at ₹419
- Current GMP: ~40%

- That's the number everyone's chasing—not the one that matters most.

🚩 100% Offer for Sale (OFS)

- Entire ₹650 crore issue is an Offer for Sale.
- Zero proceeds go to the company.
- No funding for capex.
- No debt reduction.
- Pure promoter stake monetisation.

📉 Revenue & PAT Declined in FY26

- Revenue fell from ₹779 crore (FY25) to ₹692 crore (FY26) (-11.2%).
- PAT declined from ₹112 crore (FY25) to ₹98.2 crore (FY26) (-12.3%).
- FY25 benefited from a one-time ₹222 crore parachute order.
- That was a peak year, not a sustainable trend.

🏭 Capacity Utilisation Fell to 62%

- Manufacturing capacity nearly tripled between FY24 and FY25 after the Karanj facility.
- Capacity utilisation:
  - 79.7% (FY24)
  - 84.4% (FY25)
  - 62.5% (FY26)
- Additional capex continued despite lower utilisation.
- Capacity has been built ahead of demand.

🎯 Tender-Driven & Concentrated Business

- Aerospace & Defence contributed 54.6% of FY26 revenue.
- Direct Government revenue declined from 34.65% (FY25) to 15.09% (FY26).
- Largest customer contributed 11.1% of revenue.
- Top 10 customers accounted for 59.5% of revenue.
- Business is largely based on purchase orders, not long-term contracts.

💰 Premium Valuation Despite Degrowth

- Valuation stands at around 45x P/E.
- Peer average is around 40x (Garware, Arvind, SRF).
- Peers delivered growth.
- Kusumgar reported declining earnings.
- Premium valuation leaves limited room for re-rating.

📋 CEO ESOP Increased Sharply Before IPO

- ESOP expense increased:
  - ₹0 (FY24)
  - ₹3.5 crore (FY25)
  - ₹19.9 crore (FY26)
- Majority allocated to CEO Ankur Kothari.
- ESOP scheme was introduced during the IPO preparation period.

🧾 Consultancy Fee to Former Promoter Director

- Yogesh Kusumgar resigned as Director in March 2025.
- Received ₹0.6 crore as consultancy fees during FY26.
- Nature and scope of consultancy are not clearly detailed in the RHP.

📊 Working Capital Deteriorated

- Receivable days increased:
  - 33 → 26 → 123 (FY24–FY26)
- Inventory days increased:
  - 112 → 64 → 103 (FY24–FY26)
- Both expanded significantly while revenue declined.

🏢 Related Promoter Entities

- Only one Group Company disclosed: Specialty Fabrics Pvt Ltd.
- CMD is also associated with:
  - Kusumgar Aerospace
  - Four S Weaving
  - Concord Weaving Preparatory
  - Toray Kusumgar Advanced Textile
  - V.B. Kusumgar & Co
- The June 2025 non-compete agreement covers only two of these entities.

🧭 Our Take

Listing Perspective

- Strong anchor participation could support listing performance.
- Positive GMP suggests healthy initial demand.

Long-Term Perspective

- FY25 appears to have been driven by a one-off order.
- FY26 already reflects a slowdown.
- Capacity utilisation has weakened.
- Valuation assumes growth that has yet to reappear.
- 100% OFS with rising working capital is not an ideal combination.

Bottom Line

Watch Q1 FY27 and Q2 FY27 performance before concluding whether this is a genuine long-term compounder or primarily a listing-gains opportunity.

L&T: VYOMA PARTNERS FORTANIX TO DELIVER NVIDIA-POWERED CONFIDENTIAL AI SOLUTIONS 🤝🤖

• 🤝 L&T Vyoma has entered into a strategic partnership with Fortanix to deliver sovereign, secure AI and data services for enterprises and government organizations in India using NVIDIA Confidential Computing technology. 

• 🛡️ The partnership combines L&T Vyoma's sovereign AI infrastructure (data centres, compute and networking) with Fortanix's AI security platform to protect sensitive data during AI processing and ensure compliance with India's data sovereignty requirements. 

• 🏦 The solutions are targeted at regulated sectors including banking, financial services, government and healthcare, where secure AI deployment and regulatory compliance are critical. 

• 🚀 The partners will jointly develop sovereign AI and data security solutions, pursue co-selling initiatives, conduct proof-of-concept engagements, and support Indian AI model builders through L&T Vyoma's AI Factory platform. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens L&T's AI and digital infrastructure offerings, enhancing its position in India's sovereign AI and secure cloud ecosystem.)

NATCO PHARMA: TO INVEST ₹1,400 CR IN SOUTH AFRICA ARM, RAISE ADCOCK INGRAM STAKE TO 49% 🌍💊

• ✅ The Board has approved an investment of up to ₹1,400 crore in its wholly-owned subsidiary, Natco Pharma South Africa Proprietary Limited, to support expansion in Africa. 

• 🤝 NATCO will acquire 1.96 crore shares of Adcock Ingram Holdings for ZAR 1.81 billion (~₹1,069 crore), increasing its stake from 35.75% to 49%. 

• 📊 Adcock Ingram reported US$423 million revenue and ~US$59 million EBITDA for the 9 months ended March 31, 2026. Post-acquisition, NATCO will recognize 49% of Adcock Ingram's profit after tax in its books. 

• 🌍 The acquisition strengthens NATCO's presence in South Africa and the African continent, with both transactions expected to be completed before the end of July 2026, subject to customary and regulatory approvals. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic investment expands NATCO's international footprint and increases its share in a leading South African pharmaceutical company, supporting long-term earnings growth.)

TILAKNAGAR INDUSTRIES | Records Highest-Ever Monthly Sales

The company achieved its highest-ever monthly sales of 3.4 million cases in June 2026, driven by strong demand for Imperial Blue Whisky and Mansion House Brandy.

Imperial Blue Whisky crossed 2 million cases in both May and June 2026, while also delivering double-digit QoQ growth in North India with market share gains.

Tilaknagar Industries continues to be the third-largest player in India's Prestige & Above (P&A) spirits segment and the largest P&A player in South India (excluding Tamil Nadu).

The company also expanded its premium portfolio in West Bengal with the launch of House of TI brands.

Key Takeaway
Record monthly sales and continued premium portfolio expansion reflect strong brand momentum and improving market position.

**INCRED | DIAMOND POWER INFRASTRUCTURE**

Initiated coverage with a **High-Conviction ADD** rating and a **target price of ₹300**.

The brokerage expects **plant utilisation** to increase from **~25% currently to ~60% by FY28**, driving operating leverage.

InCred forecasts **~5x revenue growth** over the next **three years**, with **EBITDA expected to grow at ~73% CAGR**.

Growth is expected to be supported by strong demand for **MV/EHV cables, underground cabling, data centres**, and **power infrastructure**.

The brokerage also noted that the **NCLT overhang has been resolved**, with the balance sheet expected to strengthen and **minimal capex** required over the next two years.

**Key Takeaway**
InCred sees Diamond Power as a high-growth play, driven by improving utilisation, strong industry tailwinds, and a healthier balance sheet.

Cleaner Fuels Are Powering India's Auto Growth

India's automobile industry closed Q1 FY27 on a strong note, with June retail vehicle sales rising 4.84% YoY to a record 20.03 lakh units, comfortably outpacing wholesale dispatches and signalling healthy end-user demand.

The biggest structural trend, however, is the rapid shift towards cleaner mobility, with alternative-fuel vehicles now accounting for nearly 40% of all retail vehicle sales. 

Electric three-wheelers led the charge, with sales surging 77.5% YoY, while passenger EV penetration improved to 4.2%, reflecting rising consumer acceptance of electric mobility.

The momentum extends far beyond vehicle manufacturers. Higher production volumes are expected to boost demand for auto ancillaries, tyres, batteries, forgings, castings, bearings, wiring harnesses, lubricants, specialty chemicals, aluminium, steel and copper.

The cleaner mobility transition is also creating long-term opportunities for battery manufacturers, power electronics, semiconductor suppliers, electric motor producers, charging infrastructure companies, industrial automation firms and capital goods manufacturers supplying equipment to the automotive and EV ecosystem.

With rural demand improving, monsoon conditions remaining favourable and cleaner vehicles steadily gaining market share, the Indian automobile sector is evolving into a broad-based manufacturing growth story-creating opportunities across the entire automotive value chain rather than just for automakers.

**TRENT | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Citi** maintained its **Sell** rating with a **Target Price of ₹2,733**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue** grew **19% YoY**, below Citi's **23% estimate**.
- **Revenue Productivity:** **Average revenue per square foot** (assuming similar new-store size as TTM) **declined 12.2% YoY**, indicating continued weakness despite a favourable base.
- **Store Expansion:** **Westside** added **1 net store** and **Zudio** added **19 net stores** during the quarter, ahead of Citi's expectations of **0** and **6** stores, respectively.
- **Seasonality:** Citi noted that **Q1 is typically the weakest quarter for store additions**, making the expansion pace less of a concern.
- **Outlook:** The brokerage remains **cautious** due to **weak revenue per square foot, rising competition, store cannibalization and continued expansion into Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi maintains a **negative stance on Trent**, believing that **declining store productivity, intensifying competition and expansion-related challenges** could weigh on future growth despite continued store additions.

**BANKING SECTOR | Q1FY27 Business Update (Motilal Oswal)**

**Key Highlights**
- Credit growth accelerates to 16.3%
- Deposit growth improves to 12.3%
- Regional banks lead performance
- LDR strengthens across the sector

**Sector Snapshot**
- **26 of 33** listed universal banks have reported Q1FY27 business updates.
- Aggregate **credit growth** accelerated to **16.3% YoY** (vs. 13.9% in Q4FY26).
- Aggregate **deposit growth** improved to **12.3% YoY** (vs. 11.3% in Q4FY26).
- **Loan-to-Deposit Ratio (LDR)** increased by **114 bps QoQ** to **86.1%**.
- **CASA balances** remained stable at **11.0% YoY**.

**Cohort-wise Performance**

**Large Private Banks**
- Credit growth improved to **16.2% YoY**.
- Deposit growth strengthened to **15.3% YoY**.
- LDR improved by **97 bps QoQ**.
- Growth led by **Axis Bank**, while **HDFC Bank** showed recovery.

**New Age Private Banks**
- Credit growth accelerated to **13.5% YoY**.
- Deposit growth improved to **11.7% YoY**.
- LDR increased by **57 bps QoQ**.
- Growth supported by **AU Small Finance Bank, IDFC First Bank, RBL Bank** and **Yes Bank**.

**Public Sector Banks**
- Credit growth improved to **16.7% YoY**.
- Deposit growth rose to **10.7% YoY**.
- LDR expanded by **105 bps QoQ**.
- Strong momentum across major PSU banks.

**Regional Banks**
- Strongest-performing cohort with **19.3% YoY credit growth**.
- Deposit growth stood at **13.7% YoY**.
- LDR improved by **111 bps QoQ**.
- Healthy growth reported by **Karur Vysya Bank, South Indian Bank, CSB Bank, Tamilnad Mercantile Bank** and **Dhanlaxmi Bank**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Motilal Oswal expects **Q1FY27 to reflect broad-based strength across the banking sector**, with **faster credit growth, improving deposit mobilisation and stable CASA trends**. **Regional banks continue to outperform**, while **large private banks are witnessing improving business momentum**, supporting a constructive outlook for upcoming quarterly earnings.

**CLSA on Industrials**

Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.

More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains

Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power

Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

**Macquarie on Industrials**

As per industry sources, government is set to exempt 4 Chinese players (detailed below), having manufacturing units in India, from the restriction imposed in February 2023 for critical power projects.

This exemption is valid for a period of two years (till June 2028)

Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.

Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out

Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up

Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

DAM Capital write up:

.HDFCBank - 1Q27 Biz Update: Better but not the best as one wanted. 

• Gross loans stood at Rs30.6 trn, up 15% YoY and 3% QoQ – highest YoY growth since merger. AUM was Rs31.3 trn, up 12% YoY and 2% QoQ.

• Deposits came in at Rs31.7 trn, up 15% YoY and 2% QoQ. **On average basis seasonal sequential pace is maintained but slower YoY, barring CASA which has base effect.**

• CASA stood at Rs10.3 trn, up 9% YoY but down 3% QoQ (from CA decline generally). **CASA ratio was at 32.3% **- This is now almost like south based regional pvt banks and with FCNR this could look more lower.

• Term deposits grew strong by 17% YoY and 5% QoQ.

• C-D ratio (on gross loans) was 96.5%, compared to 95.3% in 4Q26 & 96.0% in 1Q26.

Systematix write up:

HDFC Bank 1QFY27 business figures – as expected the CD ratio has deteriorated* 

*Advances*

Average advances under Management have grown 2.5% QoQ and 10.8% YoY to Rs. 30,386 bn

Period end advances under Management have grown 2.3% QoQ and 12.4% YoY to Rs. 31,270 bn

Gross advances have grown 3.4% QoQ and 15.4% YoY to Rs 30,610 bn

*Deposits*

Average deposits have grown 5.6% QoQ and 13.3% YoY to Rs. 30,114 bn

Average CASA deposits have grown 4.2% QoQ and 11.2% YoY to Rs. 9,570 bn

Average Time deposits have grown 6.3% QoQ and 14.3% YoY to Rs. 20,544 bn

Total deposits at period end have grown 2.1% QoQ and 14.7% YoY to Rs. 31,705 bn

CASA deposits at period end have de-grown -3.3% QoQ but have grown 9.4% YoY to Rs. 10,255 bn

Time deposits at period end have grown 4.9% QoQ and 17.4% YoY to Rs. 21,450 bn

The period-end CASA ratio stood at 32.3%, down by -180bps QoQ and -155bps YoY

The calculated Credit to Deposit ratio has deteriorated to 95.8%, up 122bps QoQ and 67bps YoY

*Valuation and Recommendation* 

HDFC Bank (HDFCB) trades at an FY27E P/ABV of 1.7x for an FY27E/FY28E RoE profile of 14.0%/14.3%. We currently have a BUY rating on the stock.
